subject: Public Health
title: Smoke-Free Laws and Their Impact
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Smoke-Free Laws and Their Impact.

hook.text: When Ireland banned smoking in workplaces in 2004, critics predicted economic disaster.

buildup.text: Smoke-free laws protect workers from secondhand smoke—bartenders and waiters breathe cleaner air immediately.

discovery.text: Within one year of smoke-free laws, hospital admissions for heart attacks and asthma consistently drop by 10-15%.

twist.text: Smoke-free laws also reduce smoking rates—denormalizing smoking changes social norms and supports quitters.

climax.text: Over 65 countries now have comprehensive smoke-free laws—protecting 1.6 billion people from secondhand smoke.

punchline.text: Banning indoor smoking didn't just clear the air—it cleared hospital beds.

quiz.question: What happened to heart attack rates after Ireland's workplace smoking ban?
